description_long: This sports center is a large, rectangular building with a prominent flat roof featuring a central skylight or glass section, likely illuminating an indoor arena or pool. The facade is characterized by a series of evenly spaced vertical columns and horizontal bands, giving it a monumental and institutional appearance. The exterior walls are primarily light gray, with darker accents on the roof and around the windows, suggesting the use of concrete and metal. The building sits on a raised platform or plinth, with steps or ramps likely leading to the main entrance. Windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical pattern along the sides, providing natural light to the interior spaces. The overall design is functional and modern, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on geometric forms.
description: A large, modern sports center with a flat roof, central skylight, and symmetrical window arrangements on a raised platform. The facade features vertical columns and horizontal bands in light gray and dark accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long facade with evenly spaced windows and a prominent overhanging roof.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance area, central columns, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, with visible window rows and roof overhang.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a simpler facade with fewer windows and a central feature beneath the roof.
attribute_entrance_type: One main entrance with double doors, framed by columns and a recessed entryway.
attribute_roof_structure: Flat roof with a large central skylight or glass section, constructed from metal and glass, with slight overhangs.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in two horizontal rows along the long facades, symmetrically aligned and evenly spaced.
attribute_architectural_style: Modernist with Brutalist influences, characterized by geometric forms, exposed concrete, and minimal ornamentation.
